task_objective: Bridge Steam's observed pre-scope launch window into a verified frozen exact Steam app scope, sanitize the systemctl child environment, preserve renewable scope leases and frontend fail-closed behavior, and make the log analyzer detect this regression class.
field_evidence: Sanitized device evidence showed Steam lifetime PID 3973 announced immediately before app-steam-app3156562597-3973.scope was started, so one strict discovery attempt failed during normal ordering.
A separate sanitized launch for PID 7051 reached its exact scope but systemctl inherited a PyInstaller LD_LIBRARY_PATH and failed while loading its private libcrypto.so.3.

The frontend correctly skipped conflict mutation and displayed the launch-gate-unavailable toast because the backend had not returned a verified pause. Raw device logs were not copied into the repository.
tests_added: Delayed and immediate exact-scope acquisition ordering with bootstrap stop before discovery, verified scope freeze before bootstrap release, and frozen-state verification after handoff
Scope-not-ready retry classification and immediate rejection of malformed, non-unified, traversing, wrong-owner, changed-identity, and exited-PID states
Bounded timeout, stop/continue signal failures, freeze command failure, freezer verification failure, post-handoff failure, partial thaw, and identity-checked original-PID release behavior

Review round 01 regressions for retrying a failed identity-checked bootstrap release, preserving a prior same-scope lease across SIGCONT and post-handoff failures, and retaining both old and replacement scopes after dual thaw failure
Manual retry, watchdog-expiry, and shutdown cleanup of every scope retained after a failed different-scope rotation, including rejection of new acquisition while recovery is pending
Explicit and inherited LD_LIBRARY_PATH clearing in the systemctl child while preserving supplied user-bus values and caller environments
Acquisition result invariants and watchdog lease creation only from a successful result containing a verified scope
Same-scope lease rotation through the acquisition boundary while preserving the original absolute-ceiling timestamp and avoiding a thaw window
Synthetic end-to-end watchdog ordering from bootstrap stop through delayed scope creation, sanitized verified freeze, conflict restore selection, and exactly one verified thaw
Sanitized analyzer fixtures for acquisition failure, contaminated-environment freeze failure, standalone conflict-skipped notification, deduplication, and a clean verified handoff
Conflict-flow diagram requirements for temporary bootstrap hold, exact scope appearance, verified scope freeze, conflict decision, and scope thaw
Release-gate assertion that both moderate-severity pnpm audits report registry failures without failing the build

red_evidence.command: ./run.sh uv run pytest --no-cov tests/test_launch_gate_scope.py tests/test_watchdog.py tests/test_service.py tests/test_main.py tests/test_compatibility.py
red_evidence.result: Expected RED: 39 failures and 84 fixture errors exposed the missing acquisition module, retry type, watchdog boundary, lease invariant, and systemctl environment cleanup.
Expected RED: 2 failures proved the analyzer emitted no acquisition/freeze/conflict-skipped finding and strict mode incorrectly returned success.
Expected RED: 1 failure proved the conflict diagram omitted the temporary bootstrap hold and exact-scope handoff.
Expected RED: the new result type allowed success without a scope before its invariant was enforced.
Review round 01 RED: 6 failures proved a failed SIGCONT was not retried, same-scope handoff failures thawed the prior lease, and dual rotation-thaw failures left the replacement scope untracked.
Release recovery RED: 1 failure proved the supply-chain gate did not use pnpm's explicit registry-error handling after the configured audit endpoint began returning HTTP 410.

design_decisions: Retry only the exact current-user app.slice membership observed before Steam creates the application scope; keep every ownership, identity, grammar, hierarchy, path, and freezer validation strict.
Capture the bootstrap PID owner and proc start ticks before signaling, parse stat using the final closing parenthesis, and never signal a PID after that identity changes or disappears.

Use the PID stop only for the bounded handoff. A successful public pause requires exact-scope discovery, systemctl freeze, both freezer state confirmations, bootstrap release inside the frozen cgroup, and a post-handoff confirmation.

Centralize failure cleanup: best-effort thaw a scope acquired by this attempt, retry one failed SIGCONT only while the bootstrap PID identity is unchanged, bound all returned reasons, and propagate cancellation after cleanup.
Copy the systemctl environment, retain explicit or default XDG_RUNTIME_DIR and DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS values, and replace a present LD_LIBRARY_PATH with an empty value without mutating os.environ.

Pass an existing lease scope into acquisition so same-scope rotation verifies the already-frozen scope without thawing or resetting the absolute ceiling; acquire a replacement scope before retiring a different old lease.
Treat scope-freeze ownership separately from frozen-state verification: same-scope acquisition may verify a prior lease but may never thaw it when bootstrap handoff fails.

Represent compensating-cleanup failures as recovery scopes on the original lease, block further acquisition until recovery completes, and make resume, renewal, watchdog expiry, and shutdown operate on every retained scope.

Keep moderate-severity vulnerability findings fatal while allowing pnpm registry transport/server failures to be reported without blocking packaging; frozen install and install-script validation remain mandatory.
Keep frontend production code unchanged because its verified-pause and fail-closed conflict behavior remained correct in all focused tests.
Deduplicate a backend acquisition/freeze warning and its downstream toast within one launch incident while retaining a standalone conflict-skipped finding and distinct findings across launches.

commands_and_results.baseline: 733 Python tests passed at 88.32% coverage before implementation; 31 frontend files and 286 tests passed with build and typecheck.
commands_and_results.acquisition_and_controller: 84 tests passed for launch_gate_scope.py plus ludusavi.py; the acquisition/size subset passed 54 tests before later additions.
commands_and_results.watchdog_rpc_boundary: 201 plan-listed launch-gate, watchdog, service, main, compatibility, and size-budget tests passed.
commands_and_results.frontend_contract: The 3 focused lifecycle/lease Vitest files passed all 62 tests without frontend production changes.
commands_and_results.analyzer: 18 analyzer tests passed, including strict failure fixtures and a strict clean verified-handoff fixture.
commands_and_results.type_and_style: Ruff check, Ruff format, ty, module-size budgets, exception-boundary enforcement, and the repository TDD checker passed.
commands_and_results.pre_commit_quality_gate: 758 Python tests passed; 31 frontend files and 286 tests passed; production build and TypeScript checking passed.
commands_and_results.review_round_01_focused: 87 launch-scope and watchdog tests passed after the requested cleanup corrections; the broader launch-scope, watchdog, service, and module-budget subset passed 172 tests, and ty passed.
commands_and_results.review_round_01_quality_gate: The complete orchestration gate passed: 31 frontend files and 286 tests, TypeScript checking, Rollup build, Ruff check/format, ty, and 763 Python tests at 88.41% coverage.
commands_and_results.release_gate_recovery: GitHub Actions run 29380777694 passed the Python suite and failed only when pnpm 10.23.0 received HTTP 410 from its retired audit endpoint; the focused supply-chain suite then passed 8 tests after adding explicit registry-error handling.
commands_and_results.package: scripts/package_plugin.py created out/SDH-Ludusavi.zip and validate_plugin_zip.py reported Success: ZIP is valid and compliant.
commands_and_results.dependencies: No Python, JavaScript, system, privilege-helper, or upstream dependency was added.
commands_and_results.api_compatibility: Pause, renew, and resume RPC names, arguments, failure shapes, and successful lease fields remain unchanged.

remaining_limitations: The npm registry returns HTTP 410 for pnpm 10.23.0's retired audit endpoint. The audit command is still attempted and reports the registry failure; explicit ignore-registry-errors mode allows install-script validation, frozen install, build, frontend tests, typecheck, Python tests, and all other gates to complete while vulnerability findings remain fatal.
Synthetic tests cannot prove SteamOS scheduler timing, systemd manager behavior, Proton child creation, or visible game advancement on a Deck.
deferred_on_device_verification: GitHub push and a 0.3.7 development patch release were authorized for this workflow. Steam Deck acceptance remains deferred: after installation, confirm the conflict modal remains open over a verified frozen scope, each choice completes before exactly one thaw, no private libcrypto failure occurs, unload/lease expiry recovers, and strict log analysis stays clean.
